message:
chore: update lint tools, verification artifacts, and beads metadata

- Update cx_first_policy lint rule for context-first argument enforcement
- Add section 10.15 bd-175t verification evidence
- Add bootstrap section bd-2zl.1 verification evidence
- Refresh beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2yvw","title":"[10.19] Implement Sybil-resistant participation controls tied to attestation/staking/reputation evidence.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.19 — Adversarial Trust Commons Execution Track (9M)

Why This Exists:
ATC federated intelligence track for privacy-preserving cross-deployment threat learning, robust aggregation, and verifier-backed trust metrics.

Task Objective:
Implement Sybil-resistant participation controls tied to attestation/staking/reputation evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Participation weighting rejects untrusted identity inflation; weighting policy is auditable and deterministic; attack simulations validate resistance properties.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/federation/atc_participation_weighting.rs`, `tests/security/atc_sybil_resistance.rs`, `artifacts/10.19/atc_weighting_audit_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-2yvw/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-2yvw/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.19] Implement Sybil-resistant participation controls tied to attestation/staking/reputation evidence.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.19] Implement Sybil-resistant participation controls tied to attestation/staking/reputation evidence." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.19] Implement Sybil-resistant participation controls tied to attestation/staking/reputation evidence." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.19] Implement Sybil-resistant participation controls tied to attestation/staking/reputation evidence." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.19] Implement Sybil-resistant participation controls tied to attestation/staking/reputation evidence." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.

 {"id":"bd-2zo1","title":"[10.21] Integrate BPET with ATC for privacy-preserving federated temporal intelligence exchange.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.21 — Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)

Why This Exists:
BPET longitudinal phenotype intelligence track for pre-compromise trajectory detection and integration with DGIS/ATC/migration/economic policy.

Task Objective:
Integrate BPET with ATC for privacy-preserving federated temporal intelligence exchange.

Acceptance Criteria:
- BPET exports anonymized trajectory summaries and consumes federated temporal priors without raw longitudinal leakage; contracts are verifier-checkable and versioned.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/federation/bpet_atc_bridge.rs`, `tests/integration/bpet_atc_temporal_interop.rs`, `artifacts/10.21/bpet_atc_exchange_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-2zo1/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-2zo1/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

- Verification for "[10.21] Integrate BPET with ATC for privacy-preserving federated temporal intelligence exchange." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.21] Integrate BPET with ATC for privacy-preserving federated temporal intelligence exchange." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:

 {"id":"bd-2zz","title":"[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.1 — Charter + Split Governance

Why This Exists:
Program charter and governance baseline: split contracts, reproducibility, and anti-regression rules for strategic integrity.

Task Objective:
Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_1/bd-2zz_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_1/bd-2zz/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_1/bd-2zz/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.

@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+# bd-2zl.1 Support Diagnostics Summary
+
+## Scope
+Support lane for `bd-2zl` (non-overlapping diagnostics/evidence only).
+
+## Findings
+1. `transplant/pi_agent_rust` snapshot directory is missing in current workspace.
+2. `transplant/generate_lockfile.sh` exits non-zero when snapshot directory is absent.
+3. `transplant/verify_lockfile.sh` exits non-zero when snapshot directory is absent.
+4. Generator script embeds dynamic UTC timestamp in output header (`generated_utc`), which conflicts with byte-identical lockfile determinism unless normalized.
+
+## Command Outcomes
+- `./transplant/generate_lockfile.sh --output /tmp/bd2zl1_probe.lock` -> exit 2
+- `./transplant/verify_lockfile.sh --json` -> exit 2
+
+## Evidence Files
+- `transplant_dir_listing.txt`
+- `generate_lockfile.stdout`
+- `generate_lockfile.stderr`
+- `verify_lockfile.stdout`
+- `verify_lockfile.stderr`
+- `generate_lockfile_timestamp_lines.txt`
